lyrics:
Yesterday I meant every word
Today I can't carry half of them
Woke up wrong, no great reason
Just one of those thin-skinned mornings
Where the shirt fits strange
And the mirror's mean
And one small comment from a stranger
Takes the whole day down with it

Some days still break me
That's just true
Some days everything I know
Falls straight through
Some days I'm steady
Some days I'm through
I can talk a good game when the light is right
But some days still break me

I hate admitting that out loud
After all the ground I thought I'd made
People say it comes in waves
Maybe that part's true
Because there are days I know myself
And days I just get through
And sometimes all I really have
Is getting through till morning

Some days still break me
That's just true
Some days everything I know
Falls straight through
Some days I'm steady
Some days I'm through
I can talk a good game when the light is right
But some days still break me

And I'm trying not to hate the part of me
That needs more mercy than I planned
Trying not to turn one bad afternoon
Into proof I am less than
Less than
(oh no)
Less than

Some days still break me
That's still true
Some days everything I know
Falls straight through
Some days I hold together
Some days I lose
I can carry myself pretty well for a while
But some days still break me

Still break me
Mm
Still break me

impression.text: This track creates a warm and intimate atmosphere, featuring a gentle texture of clean electric guitar, soft piano, and a heartfelt, forward-placed male vocal. The energy is consistently low and reflective, maintaining a laid-back, ballad-like feel driven by a simple, steady rhythm section. The production is clean and spacious, with well-balanced elements that create a cohesive and polished sound where every instrument has its own place. A defining quality is its dynamic subtlety, as seen in the transition at 0:40 where the chorus introduces subtle vocal harmonies and a fuller bassline, effectively swelling the emotional weight without disrupting the song's gentle nature.

review.review_text: Rose has always been good at the kind of honesty that sounds simple until you realize how hard it is to write, and 'Some Days Still Break Me' is that quality at its most distilled. The line 'I can talk a good game when the light is right' does more work than a whole verse of most songs, and the bridge, where he catches himself turning 'one bad afternoon into proof I am less than,' is quietly devastating. At a measured pace in A major, the production keeps everything close and warm, with the chorus earning its small swell through added harmonies and a fuller low end rather than any dramatic push. A highlight, and one that will mean something different to you on a bad morning than it does right now.
anchors_used: lyric: 'I can talk a good game when the light is right'
lyric: 'one bad afternoon into proof I am less than'
chorus swell via vocal harmonies and fuller bassline at transition point
